title: Personal OS — MuranoOS, a personal operating system, local workbench, and project foundation

fields.vision: A local personal operating system for project development, not a private life-management page.
fields.mechanism: Projects move through departments, rooms, subrooms, agents, commands, source records, task state, review surfaces, and release outputs while preserving local memory and upstream/downstream handoffs. The local build is grounded in a Tauri / React / Rust / SQLite desktop architecture that can host project workstations rather than only static notes.
fields.built_components: The Rust backend is running, the Tauri shell renders the React frontend, and the local language-model router is operational. The page and agent system is seeded, and the SQLite schema persists project state across sessions. Current frontend coverage is partial: a visible subset of the canonical page set has full UI treatment, while the remaining rooms are seeded in the backend or partially represented. The professional claim is therefore limited: MuranoOS is a functioning local foundation and a serious architecture for project development, with full department parity still in progress.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: The next build phase is to make the pipeline visible end-to-end: Nexus Lab for project discovery, department routing, instrument rooms, Arete review, source review, and release state. The longer-term goal is that MuranoOS becomes the common foundation through which new projects are generated, developed, reviewed, and published. Step 01 Department map Make departments, rooms, subrooms, agents, and upstream/downstream handoffs visible in the public description. Step 02 Pipeline wiring Connect Nexus Lab, source review, instrument rooms, Arete review, and release state into a single project-development path. Step 03 Frontend parity Bring remaining rooms to the same level of UI completeness as the most developed pages. Step 04 Production hardening Harden the inter-process command surface, model router, persistence layer, and release-state reporting.
fields.status: Status: The Rust backend is running, the Tauri shell renders the React frontend, and the local language-model router is operational. The page and agent system is seeded, and the SQLite schema persists project state across sessions. Current frontend coverage is partial: a visible subset of the canonical page set has full UI treatment, while the remaining rooms are seeded in the backend or partially represented.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: the next build phase is to make the pipeline visible end-to-end: Nexus Lab for project discovery, department routing, instrument rooms, Arete review, source review, and release state. The longer-term goal is that MuranoOS becomes the common foundation through which new projects are generated, developed, reviewed, and published.
fields.detailed_source_vision: MuranoOS is the personal operating system behind the portfolio: a local-first desktop workbench where projects are developed through departments, rooms, subrooms, software instruments, and embedded agents. The system is designed so a project can move from idea formation to source gathering, analysis, drafting, quality review, and release without losing its state, evidence trail, or next action. Publicly, the important claim is not private life-management; it is the construction of a professional project-development pipeline with real software tools, persistent memory, and explicit upstream/downstream handoffs.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: MuranoOS works like a local institutional machine for one person. The React frontend presents rooms and instruments; the Rust/Tauri backend handles command execution, persistence, agent dispatch, and model routing; SQLite preserves the records; the local-first language-model router keeps routine work on the machine unless a fallback is required.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A local personal operating system for project development, not a private life-management page.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: Projects move through departments, rooms, subrooms, agents, commands, source records, task state, review surfaces, and release outputs while preserving local memory and upstream/downstream handoffs. The local build is grounded in a Tauri / React / Rust / SQLite desktop architecture that can host project workstations rather than only static notes.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It demonstrates operating-system thinking for intellectual work: serious projects need state, roles, instruments, review loops, continuity, and local software infrastructure, not scattered chats and folders.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.
